card.intuition: Store strings character-by-character in a tree where common prefixes share nodes — searching for a word takes time proportional to its length, not the dictionary size.
card.analogy: A phone directory organized as a branching tree of letters: to look up 'Andrew', you follow A → N → D → R → E → W, sharing the 'A' branch with 'Alice' and 'Anna'.
card.time_complexity: O(m) for insert/search/delete where m = word length
card.space_complexity: O(ALPHABET_SIZE × N × M) worst case — O(total characters) with a hash map at each node
when_to_use: Autocomplete / prefix search: find all words with a given prefix in O(prefix_len + results)
Word validation in a dictionary where prefix queries are needed
Longest common prefix of a set of strings
Building block for Aho-Corasick multi-pattern search
gotchas: Mark is_end (or store a count) at word endings — without it, you can't distinguish 'app' stored in a trie that also contains 'apple'
Deletion is tricky: only delete nodes if no other word passes through them — check child count before pruning
A hash map at each node (dict) is simpler and handles arbitrary alphabets; a fixed array of size 26 is faster but only for lowercase Latin

exercise.prompt: Complete the Trie insert method. Each character becomes a child node; mark the last node as a word ending.
hints: When a character isn't in the current node's children, create a new TrieNode() and store it
After processing all characters in the word, set node.is_end = True to mark this as a complete word
exercise.test_code:
t = Trie()
t.insert('apple')
t.insert('app')
t.insert('apply')
t.insert('banana')
assert t.search('apple')  == True,  'apple was inserted'
assert t.search('app')    == True,  'app was inserted'
assert t.search('ap')     == False, 'ap was not inserted'
assert t.search('appl')   == False, 'appl was not inserted'
assert t.starts_with('app') == True,  'prefix app exists'
assert t.starts_with('ban') == True,  'prefix ban exists'
assert t.starts_with('xyz') == False, 'prefix xyz missing'
assert t.search('banana') == True,  'banana was inserted'
print('PASS')
